title: biauricula spathulata
intro:
Biauricula spathulata, also known as the spotted wintergreen, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and distinctive spotted leaves.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues and soothe irritated tissues.

The health benefits associated with Biauricula spathulata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. 

Herbal preparations involving Biauricula spathulata can include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
